content_of_post_to_comment:
Honestly not sure exactly what I'm trying to get all but I'll just tell my story and where I'm trying to head

2 months ago, a girl I was seeing that I was really into randomly had cut me off for another guy. I was heartbroken and confused over it because we never argued or anything, got along greatly and she just gave me the boot out of nowhere. Simultaneously, my job was cutting my hours back and I know soon they'd be firing me. I'm 21 years old for context and honestly reacted to this situation the completely opposite way than I should've. I honestly have based my own personal value for a long time off of whether I'm in a relationship or not, not the greatest looking guy etc so idk I just had this bad mindset which I see isn't right. Anyways, she left me randomly, my job cut my hours back and I gave up for a while. I lost my savings, time (I've spent like a month and a half just down) just all that stupid shit. I found out the girl I was seeing immediately started seeing a new guy too and it just set me off I let it all take more control over me than I should've. Along side this, one of my pets died and my cars beginning to breakdown as well. I've spent so much time down thinking about her, my car, job etc and it's just now clicking how much time I've lost over someone who didn't care about me. And how much I could've saved if I really tried.

The point I'm getting at I guess is I'm finally opening my eyes and realizing I have to build myself into someone l can be proud of and rely on. I've never been the type to typically take care of myself with stuff such as the gym, developing skills like mechanics or whatever. I haven't invested much into actually making myself into a person, l carry a lot of shame in just who I am because for years I've let other's opinions of me overrule my own self image.
To those who've gone through situations like this or just anything, how did you find yourself? What pushes you to keep going, what mindsets do you have in order to hold self love or like a self respect. I've spent many years passively and haven't really been myself fully.
